message:
fix(tui): reorder Ctrl-F/B match arms so board detail scroll takes priority over broad board row navigation

The Ctrl-F and Ctrl-B keybinding match arms for board_shortcut_focus()
(which matches both List and Detail focus) were placed before the more
specific Board+Detail handlers, causing Ctrl-F/B in Board mode with
Detail focus to move the board row cursor instead of scrolling the
detail pane content. This is the same shadowing bug that Ctrl-D/Ctrl-U
had before — specific handlers must precede broad guards in Rust match
arm ordering.

Move the board_shortcut_focus() Ctrl-F/B arms to AFTER the
Board+Detail scroll handlers so the detail pane scroll_board_detail()
takes priority when Detail is focused, and the row-level
move_board_row_relative() only fires when List is focused. Update the
comment block to explain the ordering constraint and its relationship
to the Ctrl-D/Ctrl-U pattern above.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
